approval_notes: Owner approved starting F002 slice 2 scoped to the Python worker service + scheduler service backed by a real PostgreSQL job queue, wired into the existing Docker Compose stack and canonical scripts. The React frontend is explicitly deferred to a later slice (prompt: plan approved with 'autopilot' on the proposed worker+scheduler scope).

Technology (FastAPI/SQLAlchemy/Alembic/PostgreSQL/Docker Compose/psycopg/pydantic-settings) is the owner-approved F002 baseline in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, ADR 0004, and CODEX_TASKS.md task 002. No new runtime, framework, queue library, or managed-cloud dependency is introduced; the queue is the architecture's PostgreSQL-backed queue implemented with plain SQL.

F002 is an epic; this contract intentionally covers only slice 2 (worker + scheduler). The React frontend, domain model, adapters, and Z0 classification remain out of scope and require their own increments. F002 remains failing until the full stack and its Level 2 evaluation land.
objective: Add a Python worker service and scheduler service that operate a real PostgreSQL-backed job queue, wired into the existing Docker Compose stack, database, migrations, and canonical scripts, and verified live end to end. The scheduler enqueues jobs on an interval and the worker atomically claims and completes them, with database-backed liveness health checks for both non-HTTP services. The React frontend is not added in this increment.
scope: Add apps/worker package (settings, database helpers, queue operations, heartbeat, worker loop, scheduler loop, and a health CLI) that shares the owner-approved runtime dependencies and the environment-variable configuration pattern used by apps/api.

Implement a PostgreSQL-backed job queue: the scheduler enqueues heartbeat jobs on a configurable interval; the worker claims one pending job at a time using SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, processes it, and marks it done, incrementing attempts and recording timestamps.

Implement service liveness via python -m worker.health --service <name>: it returns exit 0 only when the named service's service_heartbeat row is fresh within a configurable threshold and the database is reachable, and exits non-zero otherwise without printing connection strings or credentials.
Add apps/worker/Dockerfile, entrypoint scripts, a pinned apps/worker/requirements.txt used by the image, and apps/worker/README.md.

Add an Alembic migration 0002_worker_queue creating the job_queue and service_heartbeat infrastructure tables; it must apply cleanly from an empty database to head and its downgrade must drop both tables. These are queue/heartbeat infrastructure for the worker scaffold, not the F003 catalogue/evidence/Z0 domain model.
Enable worker and scheduler services in docker-compose.yml with database-backed health checks and depends_on postgres service_healthy ordering; the web frontend remains documented as deferred.

Extend scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 and scripts/stack-smoke.sh to assert worker and scheduler are healthy, at least one job was enqueued and processed to done, and both heartbeats are fresh, in addition to the existing API/database/migration checks.

Add pytest unit tests for pure queue-state transitions, heartbeat freshness, and claim-SQL construction that do not require a live database, and extend the requirements-sync test to cover apps/worker/requirements.txt.
Add worker runtime environment variable names to .env.example with placeholder or non-secret local defaults only.
out_of_scope: React frontend / static web application and its Docker image, static serving, and end-to-end tooling (F002 later slice).
Catalogue/offer/evidence/region domain model, immutable offer versions, evidence history, and Z0 classification (F003+).
Real source adapters, extraction, verification, and publication logic (F004+); the queued job is a no-op heartbeat, not a real scan.
Any external network access from tests, scripts, or application code, and any use of secrets in CI.
Adding a new runtime, framework, message broker, or managed-cloud dependency.
Merging the pull request, tagging a release, marking F002 passing, or proceeding to the next feature.
acceptance_criteria: docker compose config --services returns exactly postgres, api, worker, and scheduler; worker and scheduler define health checks and depend on postgres service_healthy.
Alembic migration 0002_worker_queue creates job_queue and service_heartbeat, applies from an empty database to head, and its downgrade drops both tables.

After scripts/stack-up, the scheduler enqueues at least one job and the worker moves at least one job to status='done'; service_heartbeat contains fresh rows for both the worker and scheduler services.
docker compose ps reports the worker and scheduler containers as healthy.
A live run of scripts/stack-smoke asserts worker and scheduler health, a processed job, and fresh heartbeats in addition to the existing API/database/migration checks, and passes.
Stopping and restarting the stack (stack-down without volumes, then stack-up) leaves the database and migrations intact, stack-smoke passes again, and no duplicate service_heartbeat rows are created.

With PostgreSQL stopped, the worker and scheduler health commands exit non-zero (containers become unhealthy) and recover after PostgreSQL restarts; the health output leaks no connection string or credential.
scripts/test and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit remain green; the requirements-sync test covers apps/worker/requirements.txt and fails on drift.

A fresh independent Level 2 evaluator records adversarial end-to-end results (compose services, migrations, live enqueue/process, heartbeats, health, negative database-down, restart, fresh-volume, regression) in agent-state/evaluation.json before F002 is considered for any status change.
No secrets are printed or committed; the secret-scan check remains green; .env.example contains only variable names and non-secret local defaults; no unsupported free-tier or Z0 claim is introduced.

end_to_end_steps: Re-run scripts/stack-up and scripts/stack-smoke on the branch to confirm the slice-1 baseline is green before new work.
Run scripts/test and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm the suite passes.
Run scripts/stack-up, then scripts/stack-smoke, and confirm the worker/scheduler are healthy, at least one job reached done, and heartbeats are fresh.
Stop PostgreSQL and confirm the worker/scheduler health commands exit non-zero and the containers report unhealthy; restart PostgreSQL and confirm recovery.
Run scripts/stack-down (without volumes) then scripts/stack-up again and confirm stack-smoke still passes with data intact and no duplicate heartbeat rows.
Drop the database volume, restart from empty, and confirm migrations apply and stack-smoke passes.
negative_tests: With PostgreSQL stopped, python -m worker.health --service worker and --service scheduler must exit non-zero with an actionable, credential-free message.
A drift between pyproject.toml runtime pins and apps/worker/requirements.txt must fail the sync test.
A job that raises during processing must be recorded as failed (or retried per policy) rather than silently marked done.

risks.data_migration: Migration 0002 adds job_queue and service_heartbeat infrastructure tables only; the real domain model remains F003. Migrations must apply cleanly from an empty database and be reversible.
risks.security_privacy: Worker/scheduler and the health CLI must not print secrets, connection strings, or full environment dumps. .env.example holds names and non-secret local defaults only.
risks.portability: Scripts must run on Windows PowerShell 7 and POSIX shells and resolve the repository root from their own path. The worker image targets linux and remains multi-architecture compatible.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The worker/scheduler run entirely locally via Docker Compose; no hosting, paid dependency, or free-tier/Z0 claim is introduced.
risks.regression: New services and the queue migration must not break the F001/slice-1 check suite; the queue is implemented with plain SQL and shared runtime pins, adding no new dependency.
risks.concurrency: The worker claim uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED so multiple workers cannot double-process a job; the smoke test verifies at-least-once processing to done.

notes: Owner approved the slice-2 scope (worker + scheduler backed by a PostgreSQL job queue) in the current session; the React frontend is deferred to a later slice. The F002 feature-ledger acceptance steps are unchanged and F002 will not be marked passing by this increment.
